description_long: This is a classic overhead traffic light assembly, featuring a yellow metal housing with three distinct circular signal lights arranged vertically. The body is robust and angular, with pronounced visors above each lens to shield from sunlight and improve visibility. The traffic light is suspended from a horizontal cable or wire, with a visible mounting bracket and attachment hardware at the top. The lenses are dark, likely glass or polycarbonate, and are recessed within the housing for protection. The surface of the housing shows some weathering, suggesting outdoor use, and the overall design is utilitarian, optimized for durability and visibility in urban environments.
description: A yellow, overhead-suspended traffic light with three vertically stacked circular lenses and pronounced visors, showing signs of outdoor wear.
description_view_0: Front view showing all three circular signal lights and the full width of the horizontal suspension cable.
description_view_1: Side view revealing the depth of the housing, the protruding visors, and the vertical alignment of the lights.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the back of the housing and the mounting hardware, with the cable visible above.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, similar to view 1, showing the depth and side profile of the visors and housing.
